title: Pareto frontier entry (critic-prompt evolution)
description: One line in .visionary/pareto/frontier.jsonl. Tracks critic-prompt versions that hold the current best fit on at least one scoring dimension. Future-facing: Sprint 7+ GEPA-style prompt evolution will mutate prompts and select toward the frontier. For Sprint 6 the schema + writer exist so calibration runs can start accumulating entries. A separate entry per critic-identity is the norm — craft and aesthetic frontiers are tracked independently.

id.description: ULID unique across the frontier file.

critic_identity.description: Which critic this entry's prompt is for. 'unified' is the pre-Sprint-6 single-critic prompt; 'craft' and 'aesthetic' are the split personas introduced in Sprint 6 Item 18.

prompt_hash.description: sha256 of the agent file + schema bytes at calibration time. Must equal the hash echoed by the critic's output and used by runtime calibration matching.

wins_on_dimensions.description: Dimensions where this prompt achieves the highest Spearman ρ against gold-set consensus compared to all other entries in the frontier for the same critic_identity. One prompt can win on multiple dimensions; a prompt with an empty wins list is dropped from the frontier.

sample_count.description: Number of gold-set entries used to fit this prompt's calibration. Must be >= 3 for Spearman to be computable. Sprint 6 treats <10 as 'degraded' — entries are retained but flagged.

spearman_rho_per_dim.description: Per-dimension Spearman rank correlation between this prompt's raw scores and gold-set human consensus. Null values appear for dimensions where the critic emits null scores by design (e.g. craft_measurable when numeric scorer is disabled, content_resilience when no visionary-kit.json exists).

calibration_path.description: Relative path to the calibration JSON that accompanied this prompt at the time of addition. Lets us reproduce the scoring transform later.

added_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when this entry was added to the frontier.

superseded_by.description: ULID of a later frontier entry that now holds the dimensions this entry used to win on. Written by the pareto module when a new prompt beats this one on all of its formerly-winning dimensions; used for audit rather than eviction — entries stay on disk for replay/analysis.

notes.description: Free-form annotations from the calibration run (warnings, degraded-mode flags, reviewer comments).
